steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage describes two ships that both have three decks: the Dreadnought (98 cannons) and the Caledonia (120 cannons). Only the Caledonia is first-rate. The conclusion must follow directly from this specific comparison — no speculation beyond the given data.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'not all ships with three decks were ranked as first-rate ships': Proven directly: the Dreadnought has three decks but is not first-rate. ✓
  • Choice B — 'all ships with at least 98 cannons were ranked as first-rate ships': Contradicted by the passage: the Dreadnought has 98 cannons but is not first-rate. ✗
  • Choice C — 'the Dreadnought had between 850 and 875 crewmen': That crew range applies to first-rate ships; the Dreadnought is not first-rate, so this does not follow. ✗
  • Choice D — 'the Caledonia needed a crew larger than 875 people to operate efficiently': The text gives a crew range for first-rate ships (850–875) but says nothing about operational efficiency or needing more than 875. Unsupported. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
